answer.text: Pension funds such as NPS (National Pension System)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** PFRDA (Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority) regulates pension funds in India, primarily the National Pension System (NPS) and Atal Pension Yojana (APY). PFRDA ensures the orderly development of the pension sector and protects the interests of subscribers. It is separate from SEBI (which regulates mutual funds) and IRDAI (which regulates insurance). **The Trap:** Although NPS invests in equities and bonds similar to mutual funds, it is regulated by PFRDA, not SEBI. Mutual fund AMCs can act as NPS fund managers, but the NPS product itself is under PFRDA regulation. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Many retired investors have NPS accounts. Help them understand that NPS complaints go to PFRDA, while mutual fund complaints go to SEBI. Different products, different regulators, different grievance redressal processes.
